comment_text: If u realise, every episode or ordeal has life lessons for all in a multi-generational way. 1 day the original good with success gets into his / her head becomes hubristic, while the original evil showcast the family background he / she was brought up in that resulted him / her those behaviours. A little like Batman, Joker, Penguin, 2-Faces, etc. Using Karate as a platform, Cobra Kai educates & brings across the message of indwelling and lingering sins & evil in all of us that we are no better nor wiser. We may any time fall or even become worse. Watch over our own flesh, the world and the devil. I hope the sequel continues after a hiatus.

comment_text: Great redemption arc for the Cobra Kai legacy.  One of the best series based on an 80's/90's movie series.  Each season was like an extended length feature movie.  The music, the fighting choreography, and the positive family storylines.  Like that our main villains Kreese kind of redeemed himself in the end.  He was the original Cobra in Cobra Kai, never to be fully trusted with what he would do.  It was just in his eyes.  Loved all the flashbacks back to the original movies to tell the entire story.

comment_text: Cobra Kai is a perfect example of how sequel and sequel series are done, and movies like Top Gun Maverick are proof that Hollywood if they do their work right they can make awesome sequels rather then awful reboots or remakes. I don't mind prequels, but sequels are more interesting continuation of a story. Hollywood has gotten lazy over the years and just focused on reboots and remakes for a lot of films rather then come up with original ideas for something great which is annoying.

video_title: Cobra Kai: Season 6 Part 3 | Final Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
As a fan of Karate Kid I would have to say Cobra Kai is an awesome and phenomenal sequel series. I am not just saying this as a fan and I would dare to say that this series is even better than the movies and surpassed them. The series is so well done that it gave relevance to the Karate Kid Part 3 and Next Karate Kid movies which was not well received by critics but made all the films relevant again even the remake attempt of the Karate Kid with Jackie Chan, and now the new spinoff movie Karate Kid Legends can enhance the story. Overall I think the Miyagi Do getting third place in the world championship isn't something bad. I think they should gave the bronze, silver and gold medals in the tournament.

I don't know while the series finishes off with a bang, I think there is is a lot of things that can be done to continue the story of the Karate Kid's now who are all becoming Karate Adults with their martial arts careers and professional careers in College. I wish other franchises in Hollywood would take lesson from the Cobra Kai writers to see how decent sequel or legacy sequel series are done like Disney who could do a lot better with Star Wars and the Indiana Jones franchise. It did surprise me that they jumped from going to All Valley which was likely the equivalent of being State Champion and go directly to the world championship with Sekai Tekai and not compete in the National championships. It would be interesting to see if the Karate athletes go on and compete in the Olympics in LA in 2028 for the USA national team or other state and world championships in between and what happens to Sensei Daniel Larusso and Sensei Johnny Lawrence after the world championships. I wish more franchises do stories and character development like this. Hollywood has been in a habit of making bad reboots or remakes then something interesting which continues the legacy of a franchise and does it very well done.

While this started in Youtube Red and eventually moved to Netflix, this was by far a huge surprise and phenomenal series which really did all the amazing things of combining the best elements of Cobra Kai and Miyagi Do and what both Johnny and Daniel learned from Karate from their senseis and Creese and Terry Silver the twisted villains who try to bring out their good sides even with a manipulated and flawed manner which comes out as bad. It is a shame Hillary Swank did not return to the series as it would been interesting to see her talk to Daniel. I am glad Chozen, Ally and some others did manage to return to the series though.

comment_text:
Here it is:

At 11 years old, Tyler-James from Glasgow is making waves in the Martial Arts world. From Karate and Tae Kwon-Do to Kickboxing, he is picking up medals left, right and centre!


Diagnosed with autism last year, Tyler-James has had a challenging time getting to grips with it. More so than ever before, he is relying on Martial Arts as an outlet to help him make better sense of the world. His determination has only grown since his diagnosis and now sees autism as a superpower. He wants to show other kids that they can achieve their goals too if they work hard, no matter who they are.

Tyler-James’ biggest source of inspiration is his own father Jay. After losing his eyesight to a rare condition when Tyler-James was very young, Jay has been eager to show his son that no matter what life throws at you, you kick back! Despite the new challenges he faced, he has become one of Tyler-James’ Martial Arts coaches and continues to run marathons. And it goes both ways, as Tyler-James also supports his dad by being his guide runner!

We join Tyler-James as he trains with his dad for the biggest competition of his life so far: the World Tae Kwon-Do Championships. Taking place in Scotland for the first time, Tyler-James is very excited at the prospect of competing on his home turf. Will he make the cut? What might he learn along the way?

This is a story of a boy with exceptional talent and never-ending determination, but also an exploration of a deep connection between father and son having both faced adversity, as they help support each other in their respective passions.

video_title: Cobra Kai: Season 6 Part 3 | Final Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
So we're just going to ignore the fact that everyone there- students and sensei alike- created an international incident by brawling with a weapon that got a student killed?  They're not ALL going to Spanish prison?  Spain doesn't look kindly on what they all just did.  Do the writers not know what "International Incident" means?  At the very least, they'll be returned home and barred from any and all karate tournaments for life- those involved and their dojos.  All of the Sensei would certainly get jail time in Spain before returning home.  The one who brought the weapon (Kreese) would get a very long sentence.  

This is crap that they all get to return home without any trace of a criminal record.  Kreese is an escaped convict!  How did he get a flight out there?  He gets to just walk out of prison and board a plane out of the country?   He should get to spend the rest of his life behind bars for fighting and getting a kid killed with his knife.

Writers got stupid and lazy this season.

comment_text:
Watching this show has been an absolute joy. 

One can but hope that others who want to pick up other properties from the past show the love, dedication and honesty towards the spirit of the original as William Zabka (& everyone else in Cobra Kai) has shown towards the Karate Kid. 

Never detracting from it, nor deprecating it but accepting it as a whole and telling a continuing tale within its unique world setting, where everything is resolved with an epic karate fight lol. Love it! 

Big big congrats to the champ that is William Zabka!

Meanwhile, Kreese and Johnny will make up during the tournament, leading Kreese to retire and hand Cobra Kai back to Johnny. comment_text: I was born in 84. The year the first movie released. By the time the 3rd one released in 1989 I was old enough to understand it. Going back, watching the first two and begging my parents to let me take Karate. I was obsessed with it. Eventually they caved and when I was 7 I got to join a youth karate class. Which I stuck with for 5 years. I still use the stuff I learned as part of my workout to this day. The last 6 seasons of this show have been surreal. Turning 40, right along with the original film, revisiting these characters with a new perspective of adulthood. Having children and passing on the story. Sadly this really is the end though. We're lucky to have gotten more from everyone without it ruining what came before it. It's gonna be tough to see it end.
